title: Subcutaneous Injection
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Administering subcutaneous medication. 

#### Procedure

- Confirm “5 rights” with paramedic partner, **the right**: 
- Patient
- Drug
- Dose
- Time
- Route
- Draw up confirmed medication into appropriate sized syringe: 
- blunt needle for drawing up and 25G needle to administer.

- Select an appropriate site of administration and swab the skin with an alcohol wipe.

- Gently pinch a fold of the patient’s skin at the injection site.

- Insert the needle at 45\xb0, bevel up, into the subcutaneous tissue.

- Continue to hold tissue and apply back pressure on plunger ensuring no blood is aspirated: 
- If blood appears, withdraw the needle and discard. Blood indicates insertion into a blood vessel and continuing injection may be dangerous.

- Inject medication slowly using steady pressure.

- Withdraw the needle and immediately dispose in a sharps container: 
- Do not resheath any needle.

#### Specific Notes

- Always ensure medication pre administration checks are completed prior to any drug administration.
